title: The Floating Ice Mystery
question: Why does ice float?
objective: Discover how freezing changes water's density
summary: Compare equal amounts of liquid water and ice, then use particle spacing to explain why solid water floats.

steps.title: Make a prediction
steps.prompt: Choose a prediction before placing an ice cube into liquid water.

actions.label: Predict it will float
actions.description: Predict that the ice cube will stay near the water surface.

actions.label: Predict it will sink
actions.description: Predict that the ice cube will fall to the bottom.

steps.title: Test the cube
steps.prompt: Place the ice gently into water and observe where it settles.

actions.label: Lower the ice gently
actions.description: Set the cube on the water without pushing it below the surface.

actions.label: Hold it underwater
actions.description: Push the cube down and keep it below the surface.

steps.title: Compare equal masses
steps.prompt: Compare the volume occupied by equal masses of liquid water and ice.

actions.label: Compare equal masses
actions.description: Use equal masses and note that the ice occupies more space.

actions.label: Compare random amounts
actions.description: Use different masses and compare their visible sizes.

steps.title: Build the explanation
steps.prompt: Choose the particle model that explains the observation.

actions.label: Choose the open lattice
actions.description: Connect freezing with a more open hydrogen-bonded structure.

actions.label: Choose heavier molecules
actions.description: Claim that each water molecule loses mass as it freezes.

outcomes.title: The cube rises
outcomes.message: The ice settles with part of the cube above the waterline.
outcomes.explanation: Ice is less dense than liquid water, so buoyancy balances its weight before the cube is fully submerged.

outcomes.title: The test is constrained
outcomes.message: Holding the cube down prevents it from showing its natural position.
outcomes.explanation: Your hand adds an external downward force, so this setup cannot reveal whether the ice would float freely. Failures are data: remove the extra force and retry.

outcomes.title: Mystery solved
outcomes.message: Your evidence links a larger volume at equal mass to a lower density.
outcomes.explanation: Hydrogen bonds hold water molecules in a more open crystal lattice when frozen, increasing volume while mass stays the same, so density decreases.
